name: Arrange appointments with Google Calendar and Microsoft Teams 24/7

parameters.formTitle: Arrange appointments with Google Calendar and Microsoft Team

parameters.message: =✅ You're booked for {{ $json.startTime }} — see you then!
=That slot is taken — here are 3 nearby openings.
=✅ Arrange appointments with Google Calendar and Microsoft Teams 24/7 — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### New Booking Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Email, Starttime, Service). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Keep Valid Bookings

### Keep Valid Bookings

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Check Calendar Availability

### Check Calendar Availability

Checks the team calendar for existing events in the target window — the availability check that runs before booking.

Ports: ①→ Slot Free? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Slot Free?

Two-way gate on `={{ $items().length === 0 || !$json.id }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Create Calendar Event · ②→ Propose Alternative Slots

### Create Calendar Event

Books a new event on the team calendar, once availability has been confirmed upstream.

Ports: ①→ Send Confirmation (Microsoft Teams) · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Send Confirmation (Microsoft Teams)

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Ports: ①→ Hold Until Reminder Time · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Propose Alternative Slots

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Hold Until Reminder Time

Pauses 1 hours — spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Send Reminder (Microsoft Teams)

### Send Reminder (Microsoft Teams)

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Microsoft Teams · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

Posts a chat message into a Microsoft Teams channel — same role as a Slack notify, for teams standardized on Teams.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#bookings`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Arrange appointments with Google Calendar and Microsoft Teams 24/7

**Pattern:** Meeting Scheduling · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Arrange appointments with Google Calendar and Microsoft Teams 24/7. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual booking is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Booking Form Submission → Keep Valid Bookings → Check Calendar Availability → Slot Free? → Create Calendar Event → Send Confirmation (Microsoft Teams) → Hold Until Reminder Time → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to meeting scheduling without manual effort
- Anyone automating booking in healthcare
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Improves response time
- Runs 24/7 in the background

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Availability is checked *before* booking, not assumed — creating an event unconditionally would double-book the same slot if two requests arrive close together. The confirmation and the later reminder are separate sends because they serve different moments: confirming intent now, and cutting no-shows right before the appointment.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Change the reminder lead time from 1 hour to 24 hours.
2. Simulate two near-simultaneous booking requests for the same slot and see which gets the alternative-slots branch.
3. Add a cancellation path that frees the slot back up.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your booking process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
